title: The PR #63 canon-code-sync audit gate's first real run successfully detected three pre-existing canon-code drifts in D0026, fail-closing on drift it didn't author
content: Run 25532879194 of "Dispatch Managed Agent for canon-code sync audit" on 2026-05-08T02:08-02:25 UTC against PR #63 head ea70fe4 — duration 497.17s, session sesn_011CapJE4EGQegiuaRcr2Wnk, agent agent_011CapJE373RMXnHgvyHJwJc, verdict FAIL with three findings. Finding 1 (D0026 §Dependency Direction claims "no service binding required for v1" while packages/tincan/wrangler.toml has an active [[services]] binding) was actually already fixed on origin/main but PR #63's branch was running a stale base — that finding will resolve when PR #63 rebases. Findings 2 and 3 are real on main: §Directory Structure shows tincan/ but the actual path is packages/tincan/, and the section claims worker/src/homepage.ts "is deleted" but the file is 2,081 lines and is actively routed at worker/src/index.ts:47 for GET / on ams.klappy.dev / ams.truthkit.ai. The gate's behavior is exactly what the agent-driven audit canon prescribes: it audited surfaces D0026 was load-bearing for, used direct repo observation instead of pattern-matching, and emitted a structured verdict with file:line evidence. The gate does not yet know that Finding 1 was a stale-base artifact; that's a class of false positive the operator should think about — drift findings against a PR's base might want to compare against origin/main's version before flagging, or the dispatcher should rebase before auditing.

title: Resolve Findings 2 and 3 by updating D0026 to match deployed reality — both Workers serve GET / on different subdomains; packages/tincan/ is the canonical path
content: Two paths offered by the audit agent for Finding 3: (a) delete or stub worker/src/homepage.ts per D0026's stated intent, or (b) update D0026 to acknowledge both homepages coexist. Path (b) chosen — both pages serve different audiences (developers reading about the protocol on the AMS substrate domain vs. operators minting conversations on the TinCan portal domain), and the subdomain split keeps that boundary structural rather than making it a single Worker decision. The "AMS sheds UI opinions" framing of D0026's headline still holds because the AMS homepage is a protocol-substrate intro (it doesn't talk about specific conversations or operators) — TinCan owns the per-conversation UI, AMS owns the substrate-self-description page. If the operator later decides the AMS homepage IS a UI opinion AMS should shed, that's a separate code change (delete worker/src/homepage.ts, route GET / to a redirect or 404, migrate the live-demo pieces to TinCan or oddkit's site) — not in scope for this canon-parity fix.

title: D0026 §Route Ownership and §Directory Structure updated to match deployed reality on origin/main; PR opened as a focused review unit
content: Three coordinated edits to canon/decisions/D0026: (1) §Route Ownership now lists GET / on AMS Worker (protocol-substrate landing page on ams.* subdomain) alongside its existing /v1/* / /mcp / /healthz routes; the magic-link MCP transport route POST|GET|DELETE|OPTIONS /{ns}/conversations/{alias} per D0023 is also added, since it was missing. (2) §Directory Structure path corrected from tincan/ to packages/tincan/ and explicitly shows BOTH workers' src/homepage.ts files with their respective audience labels. The false claim "worker/src/homepage.ts is deleted" is removed and replaced with an explanatory paragraph cross-referencing §Route Ownership for the audience split. (3) New trailing sentence on §Directory Structure points at packages/tincan/wrangler.toml [[services]] binding = "AMS" so the structural proof is one click away. Finding 1 (the "no service binding required for v1" stale text) is not touched — it was already fixed on main via an earlier edit; PR #63's branch will pick it up on rebase. All edits stay tier-1-stable in tone — no new architectural claims, just bringing the document's described reality in sync with the code.
